events.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
events.description: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by families A shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other. Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which was reported at 8.12pm near St. Clair Avenue West and Arlington Avenue, where the Salsa on St Clair festival was underway. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival

events.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large
events.description: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more listed as missing amid calls by president Delcy Rodríguez and UN for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has passed 4,300, the government said on Saturday. At least 4,333 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, the Venezuelan parliament chief, Jorge Rodríguez, wrote on Telegram. Thousands more people are listed as missing. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,300 as scale of recovery effort looms large

events.title: World News in Brief: UN spotlights education aid solution, Sri Lanka prison violence, humanitarian aid to Venezuela
events.description: The UN Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) urged international lenders to expand debt-for-education swaps, warning in a Friday report that many developing countries are spending more on debt servicing than on schooling their children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: UN spotlights education aid solution, Sri Lanka prison violence, humanitarian aid to Venezuela

events.title: Iran war live: Tehran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after US bombings
events.description: Iranian state media report explosions in port cities of Bandar Abbas, Sirik and Jask, as well as Qeshm Island.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war live: Tehran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after US bombings

events.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
events.description: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. By Monday morning, flooding across the city of Nanning and surrounding areas had resulted in many people being stranded on rooftops. Flood waters pose additional threats in China because of the presence of wild and farmed snakes. On Thursday local media reported that hundreds of snakes , including cobras, had escaped from flooded breeding farms. Typhoon Maysak also aided the development of two destructive tornados that swept across central China later on Monday evening. This occurred when warm air from the south, brought up by Typhoon Maysak, collided with cold air in the north. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China

events.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
events.description: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding

events.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
events.description: Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official. Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a statement. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara

events.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
events.description: Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists said, with patients enrolled just six weeks after the outbreak being declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World Health Organization (WHO) on 17 May. Continue reading...

events.title: Middle East live: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claim strikes against Oman, Bahrain
events.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed strikes against Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman in response a new wave of US attacks on Iran, state media reported Monday. The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending the war which has triggered global economic shockwaves since it began in late February. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claim strikes against Oman, Bahrain

events.title: Security Council LIVE: ‘Lost continuity of knowledge’ on Iran’s nuclear programme since US-Israel attacks, top UN official warns
events.description: Since the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February, there has been a “lost continuity of knowledge” on Tehran’s nuclear programme, Rosemary DiCarlo, the UN peace affairs chief, warned the Security Council on Friday as tensions mounted amid new strikes across the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: ‘Lost continuity of knowledge’ on Iran’s nuclear programme since US-Israel attacks, top UN official warns

events.title: At least 27 killed in fire at Bangkok pub with another 22 critically injured
events.description: The fire is one of the deadliest such incidents in the popular tourist destination in ⁠recent years A fire at a popular pub in Thailand’s ⁠capital, Bangkok, has killed 27 ⁠people, with another 22 ​in critical condition, officials said on Monday, in one of the deadliest such incidents in the tourism hub in recent years. The prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, ‌who visited the site of the fire in the early hours of Monday, told reporters that based on survivor accounts, the pub rapidly filled with smoke after a fire broke out, forcing ‌many to run to the back of the venue near the bathrooms. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 At least 27 killed in fire at Bangkok pub with another 22 critically injured

events.title: Are tanks ageing like the cavalry in WW1?
events.description: The rapid pace of technological advances in drone warfare, guided by artificial intelligence in Ukraine and elsewhere in the world, has fundamentally changed the face of conflict. There is a question about how radical that change is: an evolution or a revolution in warfare? And it's causing all sorts of headaches for governments worldwide, particularly in Europe, as they make choices about what to invest in over the immediate future.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Are tanks ageing like the cavalry in WW1?

events.title: Massive fire at Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people

Bangkok Governor Chadchart Sittipunt said the blaze spread rapidly, with smoke believed to be the main cause of death. Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause.

events.description: Israel's parliament on Sunday said it had set the country's national elections for October 27, which the latest date permitted by law. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, the country's longest-serving premier, announced that he "intends to win" the elction despite recent polls showing that a majority of Israelis want him out of office over multiple corruption trials, controversial judicial reforms, and concerns over the ongoing wars in Gaza and Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel sets October 27 election widely seen as referendum on Netanyahu's leadership

events.title: Is the most popular song played on Australian radio stations the product of generative AI?
events.description: Josh Fawaz’s song, a cover of Like a Prayer, has raised questions about how generative AI is being used in music and whether it should be declared An Australian producer has gone from little-known artist to viral sensation in a matter of months, with his hit song catapulting on to global charts and receiving thousands of radio spins. There’s just one problem: music experts and other musicians are questioning whether he produced it. They claim Josh Fawaz’s most popular song, a cover of Madonna’s Like a Prayer which reached the No 1 spot on the National Radio Airplay chart, could have been made using AI. Continue reading...

events.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
events.description: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official seals and reference numbers, had been forged by Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, who was claiming to have been appointed by the presidency to head the presidential foreign intervention promotion council (PFIPC). Continue reading...

events.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord

The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexico. events.title: Surveillance video timeline tracks accused Charlie Kirk assassin across UVU campus before fatal rooftop shot
events.description: Surveillance video shown in court tracks a figure to the Utah Valley University rooftop where prosecutors say the fatal shot was fired at Charlie Kirk.

events.title: Beloved musicians among victims in deadly Bahamas plane crash; aviation authority grounds flights

events.title: WATCH: Mike Waltz tells Cuban delegation 'this is not Havana' during heated UN speech

events.title: Iran attacks US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
events.description: Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%. More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Saharan Africa, countries spent 3.6 times more on debt than education . Continue reading...

events.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham likely died from aortic rupture, medical examiner says
events.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Donald Trump and a leading Republican voice on foreign policy, died on Saturday after a brief illness, his office said. He was 71. The District of Columbia medical examiner said the preliminary cause of death was an aortic dissection linked to arteriosclerotic cardiovascular disease.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US Senator Lindsey Graham likely died from aortic rupture, medical examiner says

events.title: Spain ex-PM draws criticism with 'xenophobic' remark on French team
events.description: Spain's conservative ex-prime minister Mariano Rajoy faced criticism at home and in France Sunday after saying the neighbouring country's national football team had "no French players". Our correspondent Sarah Morris tells us more.

events.title: More than 4,400 confirmed dead in Venezuela earthquakes
events.description: The number of people confirmed dead after the devastating back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela last month has risen to more than 4,400.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ More than 4,400 confirmed dead in Venezuela earthquakes

events.title: ‘Help is coming’: UN relief chief briefs on Venezuela quake recovery
events.description: The widespread solidarity shown to Venezuela must now be transformed into real, practical support for recovery from the deadly double earthquakes last month, UN relief chief Tom Fletcher said on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Help is coming’: UN relief chief briefs on Venezuela quake recovery

events.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first AI datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
events.description: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-hectare site in Makarewa, just north of New Zealand’s southern-most city, Invercargill. Construction is due to begin this year, with the centre becoming operational by 2028. Continue reading...

events.title: Fire breaks out at a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people, officials say
events.description: Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.

events.title: A veteran foreign correspondent looks back on a career covering conflicts
events.description: For Reporter's Notebook we hear about what it takes to cover conflict over a decades-long career as a foreign correspondent.

events.title: Thai youth leaders push for inclusion, partnership and lasting change
events.description: From local communities to the global stage, a diverse group of young leaders from Thailand is helping shape conversations on public policy, climate action, inclusion, indigenous rights, disability access and youth wellbeing.

events.title: Critics say Turkey's verbal attacks on Israel have crossed into antisemitism
events.description: Turkish FM Hakan Fidan reportedly called Israel a burden humanity can no longer bear, drawing accusations of genocidal rhetoric as Turkey hosts a NATO summit in Ankara.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
events.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

events.title: Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon title after victory over Alexander Zverev
events.description: Jannik Sinner retains his crown. The 24-year-old Italian won the Wimbledon title for the second consecutive year after defeating Germany’s Alexander Zverev in four sets. It is Sinner’s fifth Grand Slam title, his first of the year, further strengthening his position as world No. 1.

events.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
events.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian kept his composure, sealed his fifth Grand Slam crown, and celebrated by collapsing onto Centre Court in delight. For Zverev, there was still some consolation as he'll rise to world No. 2 despite the defeat. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Italian tennis journalist, Ubaldo Scanagatta.

events.title: Mathieu Van der Poel overcomes heat to win Tour de France stage nine
events.description: 31-year-old Dutchman and cobbled classics specialist Mathieu van der Poel claimed his third Tour de France win on Sunday with victory in the ninth stage, which was shortened due to intense heat. The former world champion, won a sprint amongst his three breakaway companions with Tobias Johannessen taking second and Tom Pidcock third.

events.title: World must move faster on Ebola response for DR Congo: UN relief chief
events.description: The world must move faster to contain the spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN relief chief Tom Fletcher said on Thursday.

events.title: Remembering the Srebrenica genocide: ‘Fear is all that kept me moving’
events.description: The United Nations paused on Thursday to remember the more than 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys killed in the town of Srebrenica in July 1995, and the women and survivors left to rebuild their lives in the aftermath.

events.title: Trump says 'Iran lies and cheats' as IRGC emerges as dominant force in negotiations with US

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
events.description: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 2017 a Trinidadian LGBTQ+ rights activist, Jason Jones , challenged the law, and in 2018 a high court ruled that it infringed upon his constitutional right to privacy and equality. Continue reading...

events.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: EU set to approve air travel passenger rights package
events.description: EU ministers are expected to approve a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

events.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
events.description: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

events.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy swaps out prime minister in reshuffle
events.description: Ukraine's president announced a government shake up, with PM Yuliia Svyrydenko stepping down after a year in office.

events.title: California’s largest lake is turning to dust
events.description: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

events.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
events.description: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

events.title: Ukraine allies gather in Paris to step up defence aid, heighten pressure on Russia
events.description: Ukraine's Western allies meet in Paris Monday to reaffirm their support for Kyiv and step up pressure on Russia. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky will be joined by at least 25 ​other leaders for a ‌meeting of the "Coalition of the Willing" set to focus on air and missile defence cooperation with Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine allies gather in Paris to step up defence aid, heighten pressure on Russia

events.title: Macron calls for 'constant vigilance' against antisemitism as France honors Alfred Dreyfus
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Sunday inaugurated a statue honoring Captain Alfred Dreyfus, whose wrongful 19th century treason conviction exposed deeply rooted anti-Jewish bias in France. Macron at the event decried a resurgence of the ″demons of antisemitism″ that have darkened France’s past and present.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Macron calls for 'constant vigilance' against antisemitism as France honors Alfred Dreyfus

events.title: Lindsey Graham: 'He was the one who justified Israel's actions'
events.description: Graham often advised Trump on foreign affairs, particularly on matters pertaining to Israel, Ukraine and Iran. He was a frequent visitor at the White House. Shirli Sitbon tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lindsey Graham: 'He was the one who justified Israel's actions'

events.title: Jurassic Park star Sam Neill has died
events.description: Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders star Sam Neill has died aged 78.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jurassic Park star Sam Neill has died

events.title: Ukraine's prime minister stepping down after only a year, in Zelenskyy reshuffle
events.description: Ukrainian prime minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy begins a reshuffle of Ukraine's government.

events.title: Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies - Zelenskyy pays tribute
events.description: Lindsey Graham, the US senator and prominent ally of Donald Trump, has died.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies - Zelenskyy pays tribute

events.title: US congressman 'detained by armed Israeli settlers' in West Bank
events.description: A US politician said he was detained by armed Israeli settlers during a visit to the West Bank.

events.title: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran
events.description: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran

events.title: Truck carrying wedding guests struck on Indonesia highway, killing 13 people
events.description: A truck carrying wedding guests has been struck on a busy highway in Indonesia, killing 13 people

events.title: Qatar's former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at 74
events.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at age 74
events.ticker_label: 💥 Qatar's former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at 74

events.title: Focus turns to building stronger institutions in Africa to speed shift to renewable energy
events.description: Africa’s renewable energy transition is entering a new phase as the continent shifts from proving that clean energy works to building the institutions needed to deploy it at scale

events.title: UK police release man arrested over killing of former politician Ann Widdecombe
events.description: British police have released a 26-year-old man arrested in connection with the killing of former politician Ann Widdecombe

events.title: Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close
events.description: Representatives from 12 countries carried out a “virtual diplomatic field visit” to a displacement site in the Gaza Strip and heard from some of the residents about their pressing needs, the United Nations said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close

events.title: Naked man tries to kidnap 7-year-old tourist from mother at Portland park, bystanders intervene
events.description: A naked man allegedly grabbed a 7-year-old girl at a Portland park before her parents and bystanders fought him off and officers arrived.

events.title: JonBenet Ramsey mystery reignited by lab scandal that adds pressure to unleash DNA help dad is ‘begging’ for
events.description: John Ramsey says forensic genetic genealogy could finally name his daughter's killer as Boulder police confirm the CBI DNA scandal had no case effect.

events.title: Fox News Campus Radicals Newsletter: Alleged K-12 fraud, one school's battle with Newsom and a win in Florida
events.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News Campus Radicals newsletter.

events.title: NYPD officer charged with rape, child sex crimes previously admitted to misconduct on the job, records show
events.description: NYPD officer Joshua Acosta faces rape and child sex charges after years of documented police misconduct while serving in Brooklyn, documents show.

events.title: Nicole Brown Simpson told former lover she'd 'rather die' than return to O.J. Simpson before her murder
events.description: Joseph Perrulli reveals Nicole Brown Simpson's chilling prediction about O.J. Simpson during their 1992 love affair in "The Forgotten Briefcase."

events.title: Slain New Jersey therapist’s grad school roommate recounts friendship with couple during time in Boston
events.description: A close friend and former roommate of Brooke Hanlon speaks out about the Chester, New Jersey stabbing and what she observed about Conor Hanlon during the couple's relationship.

events.title: Tyler Robinson hearing ends without ruling as evidence dismantles Charlie Kirk conspiracy claims
events.description: Prosecutors presented autopsy results, surveillance footage, and Lance Twiggs' explosive interview as evidence in the Charlie Kirk assassination case.

events.title: New Jersey woman accused of sexually assaulting child, posting video on Snapchat: police
events.description: Victoria Anne Cranmer allegedly recorded a 14-second video of herself sexually assaulting a child under 13 and shared it on Snapchat, police say.

events.title: Yale student charged after alleged sex assault on packed Manhattan subway train, attorney pushes back
events.description: Ari Shtein, 18, faces sexual abuse and forcible touching charges after an alleged assault on a Manhattan subway train, but his attorney calls it false.

events.title: Former British MP and reality TV star Ann Widdecombe found dead; man arrested for murder
events.description: A 26-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of murder after Ann Widdecombe was found dead at her Dartmoor home with serious injuries Thursday.

events.title: US urges donors to abandon UNRWA funding as UN defends agency's mission
events.description: U.S. Ambassador Jeff Bartos accused UNRWA of becoming a "subsidiary of Hamas" and urged donors to fund the Board of Peace instead at the conference.
